title: Disperma quadrisepalum
intro: Disperma quadrisepalum, also known as the African bitter leaf tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive leaves and medicinal properties. The leaves of Disperma quadrisepalum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to their reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Bioactive constituents of Disperma quadrisepalum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Disperma quadrisepalum leaves include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Disperma quadrisepalum is classified within the family Staphylocarpaceae. It is a flowering plant native to the island of Madagascar. Disperma quadrisepalum belongs to the order Celastrales and is a member of the genus Disperma. The plant is also known to be a part of the subclass Rosidae. Disperma quadrisepalum is native to tropical regions of Madagascar.
